steps.title: Treat labels as temporary
steps.content: Morales and Hale argue that labels such as invasive and native describe temporary circumstances. Because climate change can shift animal ranges and create new habitats, a label that fits now may not fit later.

steps.title: Reject unsupported timing
steps.content: Choice A invents a past Canadian population and competition by other species. Choice C says the label does not apply now, but Canada currently classifies the round goby as invasive. Choice D claims establishment even without predicted climate change, which the passage does not support. The answer is B.
